title: Limit visible metrics to the decision-critical few
summary: AI-generated dashboards display every possible metric simultaneously, overwhelming users rather than supporting decisions.
instruction: Show no more than 7 key metrics on any single dashboard view. Prioritize by decision frequency and impact. Surface secondary metrics through drill-down or dedicated views.
rationale: When everything is a KPI, nothing is a KPI. Cognitive load research (Miller's Law applied to dashboards) suggests 5-7 items is the effective scanning limit. Dashboards displaying 15+ metrics force users to find their own signal in noise.

violations: A business dashboard showing 15 metrics: revenue, users, sessions, conversion rate, bounce rate, churn, NPS, tickets, response time, MRR, ARPU, LTV, CAC, ROI on one screen.
An analytics dashboard with 12 equal-weight stat cards in a grid.
controls: A dashboard showing 5 key metrics with secondary metrics accessible from a side panel.
An operations dashboard with 4 top-level metrics and expandable sections for detailed breakdowns.
borderline: A monitoring dashboard during incident response may need more metrics visible temporarily.

exceptions.description: Executive dashboards where every metric has been explicitly approved. Compliance dashboards displaying mandated regulatory requirements.
examples: Board meeting dashboard with stakeholder-approved KPIs

detector.signalDescription: Detect metric-card patterns (number + label + optional mini-chart + optional change indicator). Count them. Flag if >7 are visible at the same viewport.
limitations: Multi-tab dashboards may show different metrics per tab.
Cannot detect scrollable metric lists below the fold.
Cannot determine which metrics are decision-critical without semantic context.

scenarios: Multi-tab dashboards loading 4-5 metrics per tab.
Operations-center wallboards designed for large displays.
mitigations: Flag only metrics visible in the initial viewport without scrolling. Check for tab/accordion patterns.

fixturePlan.description: A dashboard with 12 metric cards in a 4x3 grid showing all available metrics.
A dashboard with 5 prioritized metrics and a View All link to a secondary page.
A dashboard with 8 metrics where 5 are above the fold and 3 below (not all visible simultaneously).

notes: Normalization note: status changed from review-ready to researched. Visual feasibility is correctly assigned. The 7-metric threshold is a design heuristic, not a definitive finding. A detector can estimate metric card count from visual structure but cannot determine decision-criticality. The instruction should frame the principle ('fewer, prioritized metrics') rather than prescribing a hard number.
